steps.title: Use the one-intersection condition
steps.content: A horizontal line and a parabola have exactly one distinct real solution when the line touches the parabola at its vertex.

steps.title: Find the vertex height
steps.content:
Complete the square for the parabola:
$$x^2+10x+a=(x+5)^2+a-25$$
The vertex has $y$-coordinate $a-25$.

steps.title: Set the vertex on the line
steps.content:
For one intersection, the vertex must lie on $y=-2.5$:
$$a-25=-2.5$$
$$a=22.5$$
The answer is 22.5.
